title: leucanthemum glaucophyllum var. esterellense
intro: Leucanthemum glaucophyllum var. esterellense, also known as White Top or Estella Daisy, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and grayish-green foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Leucanthemum glaucophyllum var. esterellense include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Leucanthemum glaucophyllum var. esterellense can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or topical cream, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for different purposes.
